name: Xylose
description: Xylose is a monosaccharide of the aldopentose type consisted of five carbon atoms and an aldehyde functional group. With its free aldehyde group, it is a reducing sugar. Xylose is a sugar first isolated from wood. D-Xylose is a sugar widely used as diabetic sweetener in food and beverage. Xylose is also used as a diagnostic agent to observe malabsorption in veterinary medicine. Reduction of xylose by catalytic hydrogenation produces the sugar substitute [DB11195].
